summary: The job failed when attempting to push a branch to GitHub; the git push was rejected with a 403 permission error for the nextjs-bot account.
root_cause: git push failed because GitHub returned a permission error: "remote: Permission to vercel/next.js.git denied to nextjs-bot." (HTTP 403) when running 'git push origin update/react/19.3.0-canary-142cfde8-20260422'.

evidence.quote: Error: Command failed with exit code 128 (Unknown system error -128): git push origin update/react/19.3.0-canary-142cfde8-20260422
evidence.reason: Shows the failing command was a git push to the update/react branch.

evidence.quote: remote: Permission to vercel/next.js.git denied to nextjs-bot.
evidence.reason: Explicit GitHub permission denial indicating the authenticated identity (nextjs-bot) does not have push access.

evidence.quote: "fatal: unable to access 'https://github.com/vercel/next.js/': The requested URL returned error: 403"
evidence.reason: HTTP 403 from GitHub confirms the push was blocked due to lack of permission.
The job sets the git user to nextjs-bot, matching the identity reported in the permission error.
A token is present in the job environment; the failure suggests the credentials used for push are not authorized for the operation.
Stack trace points to the sync script performing the git push that failed.

suggested_fix: Ensure the credentials used for git push have write access to vercel/next.js. Concretely: verify the job is using a token with repo/push permissions (for example the workflow's GITHUB_TOKEN with appropriate write scopes or a PAT with repo scope), confirm the token is actually used for the git remote (e.g. set remote URL to include the token or configure git to use the ACTIONS-provided token), or grant the nextjs-bot account push access to the repository. Re-run the sync after fixing the credential/permission configuration.
